docs.title: The main message we want to spread to the world
Jesus is the Son of God who lived a sinless life.
docs.text:
• prophecied by Isaiah 53
  • Hebrews 4:15

    For we do not have a high priest who is unable to sympathize with our weaknesses, but one who in every respect has been tempted as we are, yet without sin

  • 1 John 3:5 "...and there is no sin in him."

    You know that he appeared in order to take away sins, and in him there is no sin

docs.title: Jesus died on the cross for our sins and was Resurrected
docs.text:
• Witnessed by hundres for 40 days following this resurrection (Acts 1:3)
  • Jesus did truly die on the cross
    • Why would this be important?

docs.title: Salvation by Grace through faith
docs.text:
• John 3:16 "...that who ever believes in him..."

  • Ephesians 2:8-9 "...by grace through faith..."

    For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, 9 not a result of works, so that no one may boast

  • Anything earned is never grace (Romans 4:4)

    • Now to the one who works, his wages are not counted as a gift but as his due

  • Works cannot gain you salvation (James 2 :14 - 20)

  • Works are evidence of your faith

docs.title: The Great Commission
docs.text:
• Go make desciples of all nations
  • Teach them to observe everything I have commanded you
  • Romans 1:16

    For I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes, to the Jew first and also to the Greek

docs.title: What evidence can we give to people who don't believe what the bible says about Jesus?
docs.text:
• First century historians attest to Jesus existance and crucifixion and one attests to his resurrection
  • Tacitus
  • Josephus
  • Julius Africanus
  • source
  • The gospels are eye witness accounts of their experiences with Jesus

docs.title: Consider
docs.text: When should we start talking about Jesus after we meet someone?
